title: Dungeon Godray Shader Forge
description: The user explores a dark dungeon environment pierced by volumetric light shafts. Through a shader control panel, they manipulate godray intensity, color, decay, scattering parameters, and a custom dissolve shader on the dungeon walls to reveal hidden rune patterns beneath the stone.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that loads both 'shared_assets/dungeon_warkarma.glb' as the main dungeon environment and 'shared_assets/godrays_demo.glb' as a light source prop positioned at a window or archway opening within the dungeon. Set up a PerspectiveCamera at position (0, 3, 8) looking toward the dungeon center. Use a dim AmbientLight (intensity 0.05) and a DirectionalLight (intensity 0.3) casting shadows to establish a dark atmosphere. Implement a custom post-processing godray shader as a screen-space radial blur pass using THREE.ShaderMaterial on a fullscreen quad. The shader should sample an occlusion texture generated from a secondary render of only the light source geometry against black. The godray shader must have these uniforms: lightPositionOnScreen (vec2), density (float, default 0.96), weight (float, default 0.4), decay (float, default 0.93), exposure (float, default 0.6), numSamples (int, default 100), and tintColor (vec3). Additionally, write a custom dissolve ShaderMaterial applied to the dungeon model's meshes. This shader uses a noise texture (generate procedurally via a canvas) and a dissolve threshold uniform to progressively dissolve dungeon walls, revealing glowing rune patterns using an emissive edge effect at the dissolve boundary. Build an HTML/CSS control panel with: (1) a slider for godray density 0.5-1.0, (2) a slider for godray decay 0.8-1.0, (3) a slider for godray exposure 0.1-1.5, (4) a color picker for godray tint color, (5) a slider for dissolve threshold 0.0-1.0, (6) a slider for rune glow intensity 0.0-5.0, (7) a toggle button to animate godrays pulsing automatically, and (8) a reset button restoring all defaults. Use OrbitControls for camera interaction. Validate all parameter ranges and clamp values. Animate the scene at 60fps, updating shader uniforms each frame. The godray light source screen position must update with camera movement.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  godrayIntensity: number,         // current exposure multiplier for godray effect (0.0-2.0)
  godrayDecay: number,         // radial decay factor per sample step (0.8-0.99)
  godrayDensity: number,         // sample density along radial direction (0.9-1.0)
  godrayWeight: number,         // weight of each sample contribution (0.1-1.0)
  godrayTintR: number,         // red channel of godray tint color (0.0-1.0)
  godrayTintG: number,         // green channel of godray tint color (0.0-1.0)
  godrayTintB: number,         // blue channel of godray tint color (0.0-1.0)
  dissolveThreshold: number,         // threshold value for wall dissolve effect (0.0-1.0)
  numSamples: number,         // number of radial blur samples for godrays (50-150)
  lightSourceVisible: boolean,         // whether the light source prop is visible in scene
  dungeonMaterialsPatched: boolean,         // whether dungeon meshes have dissolve shader applied
  postProcessingActive: boolean,         // whether godray post-processing pass is enabled
}
